column1: You're expecting David to ring. The phone rings. (must)
Anna is a better runner than Rula. You think Anna will probably win the race tomorrow.
A letter arrives. It's possible that it's from your cousin, Janice. (could)

column2: It/That letter could be from (my cousine) Janice.

column1: There's a knock at the door. Your mum says it might be Colin, but you know Colin is on holiday. (can't)

column2: It can't be Colin (because he's on holiday).

column1: You see someone wearing a costume. You think there's a strong probability that she's going to a fancy-dress party. (must)

column2: She must be going to a fancy-dress party.

column1: Your sister is looking for her hairbrush. You think it's possible that it's in the living room. (might)

column2: It/Your hairbrush might be in the living room.

column1: You're waiting for Harriet to arrive. You think she'll probably be there in half an hour. (should)

column2: Harriet/She should be here in half an hour.

column1: You're talking about why James seems to be bad. You think it's possible he's in trouble at school. (could)

column2: James/He could be in trouble at school.
